item.title: Secrets export/import and encrypted storage backend
steps: Create src/security/secrets/store.ts with SecretStore interface: get(name), set(name, value), delete(name), list(), export(), import()
Implement PlaintextEnvStore as SecretStore wrapping existing env.ts parseEnv/serializeEnv — this is the default backend
Implement EncryptedStore as SecretStore: secrets encrypted with AES-256-GCM via Node.js crypto, master key derived from user passphrase via scrypt, stored in .env.enc
Add `secrets export` sub-subcommand: prompt for encryption passphrase, encrypt all secrets to portable archive (.clawhq-secrets.enc), include metadata and audit trail
Add `secrets import` sub-subcommand: prompt for passphrase, decrypt archive, verify integrity (HMAC), merge or overwrite into current .env with conflict prompts
Add `--encrypted` flag to `clawhq secrets` to enable encrypted backend (migrates existing .env to .env.enc, securely wipes plaintext .env)
Integrate with deploy pipeline: decrypt-to-tmpfs at deploy time (mount secrets as tmpfs volume, decrypt on container start, never write plaintext to disk)
Write unit tests for encryption round-trip, export/import cycle, backend switching
acceptanceCriteria: `clawhq secrets export` creates encrypted archive of all secrets with passphrase protection
`clawhq secrets import` restores secrets from encrypted archive with integrity verification
SecretStore interface abstracts storage — plaintext .env and encrypted backends are interchangeable
`--encrypted` flag migrates from plaintext to encrypted storage and securely wipes old .env
Deploy pipeline decrypts to tmpfs (secrets never written to persistent disk in encrypted mode)
